summary: Build a treatment-analysis chatbot where users provide patient symptoms and the bot suggests possible medicines/treatment guidance.
architectural_notes: Healthcare decision-support chatbot: user enters symptoms; system outputs suggested medicines/treatment guidance.

existing_features.title: Create a chatbot-style UI where a user can enter a patient's symptoms as free text, submit them, and see a chronological chat transcript of user messages and assistant replies.

Add safety guardrails to all chatbot responses: include a visible medical disclaimer, include 'seek urgent care' red-flag guidance when dangerous symptoms are detected, and avoid dosing instructions that require patient-specific factors unless explicitly provided.
Connect the frontend chat UI to the backend treatment-analysis method using the existing actor/React Query patterns in the template, including loading and error states.

Provide a simple onboarding/intake step that encourages users to include key context (age group, pregnancy status, duration, severity, allergies, current meds, chronic conditions) before the analysis is generated.
Define and apply a coherent visual theme (colors, typography, layout) appropriate for a healthcare assistant, and use it consistently across the app screens without using a blue/purple palette.

new_feature_requests.summary: Chatbot accepts patient symptoms and provides medicine/treatment suggestions (physician-style guidance)
Implement a backend Motoko service that accepts a symptom description (text) and returns treatment guidance using a deterministic, rule-based clinical decision mapping (no external APIs/LLMs).

projectName: Patient symptom intake chatbot with rule-based treatment suggestions
